meta.title: Armenia LLM Summer School 2026 — Funding Request

slide1.headline: Building Armenia’s LLM talent pipeline
slide1.subHeadline: with a world-class annual summer school
slide1.intro: The 3rd edition (Aug 3–7, 2026) brings together students, researchers, and engineers for lectures, practical sessions, and a 24-hour hack day (Aug 8) with our partner AI9.
whatWeDeliverBullets: High-signal lectures on modern LLM training, post-training, and safety
Hackathon to convert learning into prototypes and community projects
Access + inclusion via fee waivers for students and exceptional applicants
slide1.whatWeDeliverNote: We’re seeking donor support to expand scholarships, improve hybrid delivery, and sustain compute + operations for 2026.
atAGlanceBullets: GPU access provided for in-person hands-on sessions
slide1.footerSource: Source: ArmLLM 2026 FAQ + past-edition datasets in this repo (see `2026/*.xlsx`).

slide2.title: Demand is strong
slide2.subtitle: Applications vs capacity (2025)
slide2.headline: Interest far exceeds seats
slide2.body: Our selection is competitive. Donor funding directly expands scholarship support and improves our ability to serve qualified applicants.
slide2.whoAppliesNote: Applicant backgrounds include both academia and industry. Selection is competitive and includes a technical assessment (Python skills test).

slide3.title: Quality and outcomes
slide3.subtitle: Participant feedback (2024)
slide3.headline: Participants rate the program highly
slide3.body: Feedback from the inaugural edition shows strong satisfaction with lecture quality and instructor effectiveness — a key signal for sustaining and scaling the program.
bullets: Hands-on sessions are a core value: 4.11/5 average rating
Strong repeatable format: lectures + practicals + hackathon

slide4.title: World-class speakers
slide4.subtitle: Faculty from top labs and universities
slide4.headline: Faculty quality is our cornerstone
slide4.body: Past editions featured speakers affiliated with Meta FAIR, Google Research, Amazon, MIT, USC, UCL, MILA, and AI2. The curriculum spans pretraining, post-training, multimodality, agents, security, and safety.
slide4.col1Note: 10 listed speakers (2024) in our structured program data.
slide4.col2Note: 5 listed speakers (2025) in our structured program data.
slide4.col3Note: We keep the program current with the field.

slide5.title: Organizers + partners
slide5.subtitle: Annual commitment since 2024
slide5.headline: A committed team, year over year
slide5.body: The summer school is organized by researchers and practitioners across academia and industry, with partners supporting venue and compute.
slide5.organizersNote: Organizers are maintained as structured data per edition.
slide5.mediaNote: Donors receive meaningful visibility to a targeted AI audience.

slide6.title: Funding request (2026)
slide6.subtitle: What your support enables
slide6.headline: We’re seeking donor support for 2026
slide6.body: Our goal is to expand access (waivers) and sustainably run an annual program that keeps Armenia’s AI talent connected to frontier work.
whereFundsGoBullets: Scholarships / fee waivers for students and exceptional applicants
